items.title: Vijf voor twaalf bij Heracles: 'Natuurlijk maak ik me zorgen'
items.summary: Het ziet er niet goed uit voor Heracles Almelo in de Eredivisie. De ploeg staat onderaan met slechts veertien punten en een doelsaldo van min 22. De Almeloërs hebben weinig aanknopingspunten voor de komende weken en trainer Ernest Faber maakt zich dan ook zorgen.

items.title: Monaco's Balogun: Playing Real Madrid 'a dream'
items.summary: United States men's national team and Monaco striker Folarin Balogun told ESPN he will achieve one of his dreams on Tuesday when the Ligue 1 club face Real Madrid at the Bernabéu in the Champions League.

items.title: Sources: Marseille chase Arsenal teen Nwaneri
items.summary: Marseille are one of several clubs exploring a loan move for Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Source: Sporting KC majority stake sold at $700M
items.summary: A minority owner in MLS side Sporting Kansas City has completed a deal to purchase a majority stake in the club from its current owners, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: VI Live: Champions League ontwaakt uit korte winterslaap
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van dinsdag 20 januari. De Champions League gaat na een korte winterstop weer verder! We houden je hier op de hoogte van de ontwikkelingen. De VI Live van Villarreal - Ajax vind je hier .

items.title: Het RKC Waalwijk van Spanje knokt zich altijd weer terug
items.summary: Het Estadio de la Cerámica, met zijn kenmerkende gele gevel, is een van de elementen die Villarreal tot Villarreal maken. Maar er zijn meer typische kenmerken voor de club, dinsdagavond de tegenstander van Ajax in de Champions League.

items.title: David Squires on … Frank and the Spurs supertanker getting in a tight spot again
items.summary: Our cartoonist on the latest manager in north London to take the good ship Tottenham in the wrong direction Buy a cartoon | David’s favourite work of 2025 And his latest book, Chaos in the Box: get it now Continue reading...

items.title: 'Schalke wil stunten en met Dzeko (39) koers zetten naar Bundesliga'
items.summary: Schalke 04 wil zich spoedig versterken met Edin Dzeko (39). De voormalige topspits moet de Duitse traditieclub helpen te promoveren naar de Bundesliga. Dat melden diverse bronnen in Duitsland.

items.title: Defending champions Sinner and Keys advance in Australia; Boulter, Kartal exit
items.summary: Defending Australian Open champions Jannik Sinner and Madison ‍Keys advanced in Melbourne as Brits Sonay Kartal and Katie Boulter bowed out.

items.title: Haller worstelt bij dolend FC Utrecht: 'Ik wil zo graag iets moois laten zien'
items.summary: De terugkeer van Sébastien Haller werd afgelopen zomer luid bejubeld door - een groot deel van - de aanhang van FC Utrecht, maar de spits heeft zijn stempel dit seizoen nog nauwelijks kunnen drukken. Nu de resultaten ook nog vies tegenvallen, raakt dat Haller nog meer.

items.title: Dominik Szoboszlai reveals ‘no decision’ reached over new Liverpool contract
items.summary: Midfielder in talks to extend deal beyond summer 2028 He denies error against Barnsley was show of disrepesct Dominik Szoboszlai has confirmed talks are progressing over a new contract at Liverpool but “no decision” has been reached over his future. The Hungary captain has been Liverpool’s standout performer this season and is under contract at the club until the summer of 2028. The Premier League champions are keen to reward the midfielder’s progress since he arrived in a £60m deal from RB Leipzig in July 2023 and consider him a potential future captain. The 25-year-old insists he would be happy to stay at Anfield with the caveat that there is always uncertainty in football. Continue reading...

items.title: Bournemouth sign midfielder Toth for £10.4m
items.summary: Bournemouth sign Hungary international Alex Toth for £10.4m from Ferencvaros, with the midfielder joining the Cherries on a five-and-half-year contract.

items.title: Salah future down to manager and club - Szoboszlai
items.summary: Dominik Szoboszlai spoke to Mohamed Salah "almost every day" when he was at Afcon but refuses to speculate on the Egypt forward's future at Liverpool.

items.title: Football transfer rumours: Liverpool in for Van de Ven? Troy Parrott to Leeds?
items.summary: Today’s rumours are playing the long game As the vultures circle the Tottenham misery hole, there’s talk that Liverpool want to pluck Micky van de Ven from Thomas Frank’s injury-ravaged and form-deficient squad. Weekend whispers had it that Liverpool were preparing a £78m bid for the Dutch defender, though a move would be likelier in the summer. By way of return, and more immediately, Spurs have shown interest in luring Curtis Jones from Anfield and Juventus could be in for Liverpool’s Federico Chiesa for a fee of around £13m-£17m. Gabriel Jesus could be on the move from Arsenal back to Palmeiras, where the striker began his career. The Brazilian, though, says he wants a new deal with the Premier League leaders, after an injury-ravaged period, but a move could happen in the summer if not now. An Emirates Stadium arrival could be Federico Dimarco. The Italian outlet Tuttosport reports “genuine interest” from the Gunners in the Inter full-back, whose deal at San Siro ends in June next year. Continue reading...

items.title: 'You get used to losing!' - Lowry's plan to bounce back from Dubai 'hurt'
items.summary: Shane Lowry believes he can take plenty of learnings into the Hero Dubai Desert Classic after being left 'hurt' by his final-hole disappointment at the Dubai Invitational on Sunday.

items.title: Brahim Díaz’s nightmare miss shows dangers of trying to emulate Panenka
items.summary: While the famous penalty technique is the ultimate act of showmanship, the cost of failure is too high to justify Being too smart for your own good is usually drummed out of children before they leave school but sometimes people cannot help themselves. The Panenka penalty, successfully executed, offers the limited benefit of making a goalkeeper look silly and the taker a genius but Brahim Díaz is the latest to learn the cost of what happens when it goes wrong. Díaz was given 15 minutes to consider what to do with his spot-kick after the ludicrous levels of drama in the Africa Cup of Nations final. Maybe this was his undoing: being able to ponder every option, from the rudimentary to the artistic, until deciding to replicate Antonin Panenka’s creation with what could, and should, have been the last kick of the tournament. Continue reading...

The day I discovered the Everton contract Revie never signed
items.summary: This is the story of how an unsigned contract that lay hidden in a bungalow for more than 50 years could have changed the course of English football.

items.title: Why Premier League clubs keep signing each other's players for the same fee
items.summary: Premier League clubs are doing business with each other at an unusually higher rate this season.

items.title: Why clamor for Man United, Tottenham 'DNA' makes no sense
items.summary: Fans of Man United and Spurs expect a certain style of play. Nobody complains when their team is winning.
